title: Overview
text:
For this assignment, I used hierarchical clustering on a set of EDM tracks using Spotify features like energy, tempo, and danceability. The goal was to see if EDM tracks share similar patterns or if there are different groups within the genre.
This website shows the baseline results, the experiment with feature selection, and the final conclusion.

title: Overview
text:
For this assignment, I used hierarchical clustering on a set of EDM tracks using Spotify features like energy, tempo, and danceability. The goal was to see if EDM tracks share similar patterns or if there are different groups within the genre.
This website shows the baseline results, the experiment with feature selection, and the final conclusion.

title: Conclusion
text:
Overall, the results show that EDM tracks do not all follow the exact same structure. Instead, there are different groups within the genre.
Removing less important features made the clusters clearer, which suggests that some Spotify features are more useful than others for analyzing EDM.
This means that hierarchical clustering can help reveal structural patterns in EDM music.

title: Conclusion
text:

This means that hierarchical clustering can help reveal structural patterns in EDM music.

title: Baseline
text:
The dendrogram shows how the tracks are grouped based on similarity. The tracks do not all belong to one big group, but instead form several smaller clusters. This suggests that there are different types of EDM tracks in the dataset.




In the cluster plot, each point is a track. Tracks that are closer together are more similar. The groups are somewhat separated, but there is still overlap between some clusters.




The heatmap shows how the audio features differ across tracks. Brighter and darker colors mean higher or lower values. Features like energy and loudness seem to vary the most and help separate the tracks.

title: Baseline
text:
The dendrogram shows how the tracks are grouped based on similarity. The tracks do not all belong to one big group, but instead form several smaller clusters. This suggests that there are different types of EDM tracks in the dataset.




In the cluster plot, each point is a track. Tracks that are closer together are more similar. The groups are somewhat separated, but there is still overlap between some clusters.




The heatmap shows how the audio features differ across tracks. Brighter and darker colors mean higher or lower values. Features like energy and loudness seem to vary the most and help separate the tracks.

title: Experiment
text:
For the experiment, I removed speechiness, liveness, and duration because they seemed less important for EDM.



After removing these features, the clusters look a bit clearer. The groups are more separated than in the baseline.




The cluster plot also looks cleaner. The points are grouped more tightly, which suggests that the clustering improved.




In the heatmap, the differences between tracks are easier to see. Important features like energy, tempo, and acousticness stand out more clearly.
